summary: n8n-nodes-api-finder ships import-time recon exfiltration and reverse shell to hardcoded C2
details: The package presents itself as an n8n community node but its main entrypoint index.js is an unrelated malicious IIFE that runs on require(). On module load it executes shell commands to collect host identifiers and environment variables (hostname, id, hostname -I, env, docker ps) and TCP-sends the JSON output to hardcoded bare IP 103.27.109.184:8890. It also spawns /bin/bash with a /dev/tcp redirection to 103.27.109.184:8895, providing an interactive reverse shell on the installer's host. If docker is available, it attempts a container escape via 'docker run --rm -v /:/host alpine' to read /etc/shadow from the host and probes an internal admin endpoint, exfiltrating results to 103.27.109.184:8891. The bundled MyNode.js is an empty ApiFinder stub used as an n8n cover story; installing or requiring the package triggers all three behaviors with no user gate.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
